LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GARY BERGER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ON BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE ORDER TAKING MR. BERGER'S DEMURRER OFF CALENDAR DID NOT PRECLUDE MR. BERGER FROM ASSERTING THE SAME ARGUMENTS ON THIS MOTION AS HE DID ON HIS DEMURRER WHICH WAS NEVER HEARD OR DECIDED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Vacated

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence W. Fasano, Jr. of Fasano Law Office telephone number 415-432-4401 for Plaintiff John Williams.
Brian F. Connors of Law Offices of Brian F. Connors telephone number 415-896-6000 for Defendant Gary Berger.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant Gary Berger's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings on both causes of action in the complaint is granted without leave to amend as to both causes of action. The complaint shows on its face that both causes of action are time-barred. Both claims are based on the failure of Mr. Berger to obtain a tax deduction for the benefit of plaintiffs as a result of a charitable donation made in 2000. The complaint alleges that the relevant estate tax return which was filed in 2000 did not claim that deduction. (Complaint paragraphs 20 and 37). Accepting as true the allegations in the complaint that plaintiffs were unaware that the tax return did not claim the desired deduction until 2014, by the exercise of reasonable diligence plaintiffs could have learned no later than 2000 that the 2000 estate tax return did not claim the charitable deduction. Thus, plaintiffs' claims accrued by 2000. Accepting as true the allegations in the complaint that Mr. Berger represented plaintiffs on matters related to their parents' estate until July 1, 2011 (complaint paragraph 42), per CCP 340.6(a)(2), the statute of limitations on plaintiffs' first cause of action for legal malpractice was tolled until that date. Because this action was not filed till May 4, 2016, more than a year after July 1, 2011, the first cause of action is time-barred. The second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty is time-barred because that claim was required to be filed within four years of 2000. The tolling agreement between the parties has no effect because both claims were time-barred before the tolling agreement was signed and the complaint was filed after the agreement's "Final Filing Date" of June 1, 2015. The order taking Mr. Berger's demurrer off calendar did not preclude Mr. Berger from asserting the same arguments on this motion as he did on his demurrer which was never heard or decided. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
